title.en: Handling Unsolicited Advice: "Thanks for the concern, I'll handle it."
title.ko: 걱정해 주셔서 감사해요. 제가 알아서 잘 해볼게요 (참견에 대처하기)

description.en: Dealing with "Ojirap" (nosy interference)? Learn how to set a firm boundary while acknowledging their intent with the phrase "제가 알아서 할게요."
description.ko: 사생활에 대한 지나친 참견이나 조언을 받았을 때! 상대방의 기분을 상하게 하지 않으면서 내 의사를 정중하고 단호하게 밝히는 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Social. Boundary. Privacy. Manners. Assertiveness. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina, I love the community feel in Korea, but sometimes people give me advice I didn't ask for.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Like, a neighbor told me I should wear warmer clothes or asked why I’m not married yet. I know they mean well, but it feels like they’re crossing a line.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아! 한국에서 흔히 말하는 오지랖 때문에 당황하셨군요! 사실 상대방은 도움을 주고 싶어서 하는 말인 경우가 많아요.
content.translation: Ah, you were flustered by what we commonly call 'Ojirap' in Korea! Actually, the other person often says those things because they want to help.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 하지만 그 말이 불편하다면, 상대방의 마음은 받되 내 선은 확실히 지키는 리액션이 필요해요. 바로..., "걱정해 주셔서 감사해요. 그래도 제가 알아서 잘 해볼게요" 라고 말이죠.
content.translation: But if those words are uncomfortable, you need a reaction that accepts their heart but clearly keeps your line. Like saying, "Thanks for the concern. I'll take care of it myself."

content.speaker: John
content.text: "제가 알아서 할게요"? Isn't that a bit strong?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그래서 앞에..., "걱정해 주셔서 감사해요" 라는 완충제를 넣는 게 중요해요. 그러면..., "당신의 관심은 고맙지만 이 일의 주인은 나다" 라는 의미가 정중하게 전달되거든요.
content.translation: That's why it's important to put the buffer "Thanks for worrying about me" in front. Then, it politely conveys the meaning, "I appreciate your interest, but I am the owner of this matter."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘은 웃는 얼굴로 내 사생활을 지키는 '철벽 리액션' 들을 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Today, we'll learn 'iron-wall reactions' to protect your privacy with a smile!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 걱정해 주셔서 감사해요. 제가 알아서 잘 해볼게요.

content.speaker: John
content.text: This sounds like I’m ending the topic for good.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. "알아서 하겠다" 는 말은..., "내가 책임지고 잘 하겠다" 는 뜻이라서 상대방이 더 이상 참견하기 어렵게 만들죠.
content.translation: Right. Saying "I'll do it on my own" means "I'll take responsibility and do it well," so it makes it hard for the other person to interfere anymore.

insight.title: 제가 알아서 잘 해볼게요
insight.description: 'I will take care of it myself'
insight.usage_tip: 제가(I/Polite) + 알아서(On my own/Discretion) + 잘 해볼게요(Will try to do it well). This phrase emphasizes your autonomy and competence.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What if they keep pushing their opinion?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐..., "네 참고할게요" 라고 짧게 답해 보세요.
content.translation: In that case, try answering briefly, "Yes, I'll take that into account."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "당신의 의견을 들었다" 는 표시만 하고 대화를 마무리하는 아주 유용한 리액션이에요.
content.translation: It's a very useful reaction that just signals "I heard your opinion" and ends the conversation.

insight.title: 참고할게요
insight.description: 'I will take it into account / I'll refer to it'
insight.usage_tip: 참고(Reference) + 할게요(Will do). This is a polite way of saying "I heard you, but I'll decide whether to follow it or not."

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. I'm at a neighborhood gathering, and an older neighbor is giving me unsolicited advice about my career.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존! 요즘 세상이 얼마나 험한데 그렇게 프리랜서로만 일해서 되겠어? 얼른 안정적인 큰 회사에 들어가야지! 내가 아는 곳 소개해 줄까?
content.translation: John, the world is so tough these days, is it okay to just work as a freelancer like that? You should quickly enter a stable large company! Shall I introduce you to a place I know?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 아! 걱정해 주셔서 정말 감사합니다. 안 그래도 요즘 많이 고민하고 있어요.
content.translation: Ah, thank you so much for worrying about me. I've been thinking about it a lot lately too.

insight.title: 걱정해 주셔서 정말 감사합니다
insight.usage_tip: Starting with gratitude makes your following boundary sound less defensive.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 고민만 하면 안 돼! 지금 바로 연락해 봐야지. 내 말대로 하는 게 좋을 거야.
content.translation: You shouldn't just think about it! You should call right now. It'll be good to do as I say.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 좋은 말씀 감사합니다. 하지만 제 일은 제가 알아서 잘 해볼게요. 필요하면 나중에 꼭 여쭤볼게요!
content.translation: Thank you for the good words. But I'll take care of my own work myself. I'll definitely ask you later if I need help!

insight.title: 제가 알아서 잘 해볼게요
insight.usage_tip: By adding '제 일은' (My matter/work), John clearly defines the boundary of his personal responsibility.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아... 그래요? 존 씨가 생각이 다 있겠지. 그래 잘 해봐요!
content.translation: Ah... is that so? I'm sure you have your own thoughts, John. Yes, do well!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 네! 관심 가져 주셔서 감사해요.
content.translation: Yes! Thank you for your interest.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Wow, once I said "알아서 할게요," the conversation shifted back to me being the person in charge. It felt great!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 상대방의 배려를 거절하는 게 아니라, 내 삶의 주인으로서 정중하게 리액션하는 게 중요하답니다.
content.translation: Right. It's not about rejecting the other person's consideration; it's about reacting politely as the owner of your life.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "boundary-setting" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 걱정해 주셔서 감사해요. 제가 알아서 잘 해볼게요!
content.translation: Thanks for the concern. I'll take care of it myself!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 좋은 말씀 감사합니다. 제가 참고할게요!
content.translation: Thank you for the good words. I'll take that into account!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 마음 써 주셔서 정말 감사합니다!
content.translation: Thank you so much for your kind thoughts!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 나중에 도움 필요하면 말씀드릴게요!
content.translation: I'll let you know if I need help later!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 지금 제 상황에 만족하고 있어서요!
content.translation: Because I'm satisfied with my situation right now!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 누군가의 참견에 당황하지 말고, 여러분의 선을 정중하고 당당하게 리액션해 보세요.
content.translation: Everyone, don't be flustered by someone's interference; try reacting to your line politely and confidently.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "걱정해 주셔서 감사해요. 제가 알아서 잘 해볼게요."
content.translation: Thanks for the concern. I'll take care of it myself.

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. Only one episode left for Season 2!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 우리 마지막 30화에서 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you in our last episode, number 30. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 오지랖이 넓은 사람은 남의 일에 참견을 많이 해요. (A nosy person interferes a lot in other people's business.)

vocabulary.meaning: To do it on one's own / At one's discretion
vocabulary.example: 숙제는 누가 시키지 않아도 알아서 해야 해요. (You should do your homework on your own even if no one tells you to.)

vocabulary.meaning: To refer to / To take into account
vocabulary.example: 선생님의 조언을 참고해서 계획을 세웠어요. (I made a plan by taking the teacher's advice into account.)
그는 이제 안정적인 직장을 갖고 싶어 해요. (He wants to have a stable job now.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be firm / To be resolute
vocabulary.example: 그녀는 자신의 뜻을 단호하게 밝혔어요. (She stated her intention firmly.)
그의 행동은 비록 실수였지만 선의에서 나온 것이었어요. (Although his action was a mistake, it came from good intentions.)
